Title: Thirteen Dipterocarpoideae genomes provide insights into their evolution and borneol biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase (TPS); monoterpene cyclase; contains conserved RRX8W, DDXXD, and NSE/DTE motifs
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Bornyl diphosphate; borneol detected after alkaline phosphatase dephosphorylation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes geranyl diphosphate to bornyl diphosphate, which yields borneol after dephosphorylation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms immediate precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal monoterpene used for anxiety, pain, anesthesia; contributes to essential oil bioactivity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Essential oils; damar resin and oils of Dipterocarpaceae species
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in E. coli BL21(DE3)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes borneol biosynthesis with very high product specificity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DiTPS2 was one of five Dipterocarpus TPS candidates that produced borneol from GPP in vitro. 2. The enzyme showed high specificity, generating borneol with almost no other intermediate monoterpenes detected. 3. It was identified as a functional BPPS supporting borneol biosynthesis in Dipterocarpus.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Motif filtering; heterologous protein expression in E. coli; in vitro enzyme assay with GPP; alkaline phosphatase treatment; GC-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key enzyme for borneol biosynthesis; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzymatic assay; alkaline phosphatase added to dephosphorylate bornyl diphosphate for GC-MS detection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for pathway reconstruction and metabolic engineering for natural borneol/monoterpenoid production due to high specificity
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing; RNA-seq; PacBio Iso-Seq; CNGBdb CNP0002104
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene name prefix indicates origin from D. intricatus; paper functionally classifies it among BPPS genes although named TPS.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase (TPS); monoterpene cyclase; contains conserved RRX8W, DDXXD, and NSE/DTE motifs
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Bornyl diphosphate; borneol detected after alkaline phosphatase dephosphorylation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes geranyl diphosphate to bornyl diphosphate, which yields borneol after dephosphorylation

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in E. coli BL21(DE3)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes borneol biosynthesis with very high product specificity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DiTPS3 catalyzed borneol formation from GPP in vitro. 2. The product spectrum was highly specific for borneol with almost no by-products. 3. It is a functional BPPS from Dipterocarpus.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Motif filtering; heterologous protein expression in E. coli; in vitro enzyme assay with GPP; alkaline phosphatase treatment; GC-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key enzyme for borneol biosynthesis; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzymatic assay; alkaline phosphatase added to dephosphorylate bornyl diphosphate for GC-MS detection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-specificity BPPS valuable for synthetic biology and borneol production engineering
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing; RNA-seq; PacBio Iso-Seq; CNGBdb CNP0002104
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene name prefix indicates origin from D. intricatus.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase (TPS); monoterpene cyclase; contains conserved RRX8W, DDXXD, and NSE/DTE motifs
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Bornyl diphosphate; borneol detected after alkaline phosphatase dephosphorylation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes geranyl diphosphate to bornyl diphosphate, which yields borneol after dephosphorylation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms immediate precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal monoterpene used for anxiety, pain, anesthesia; contributes to essential oil bioactivity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Essential oils; damar resin and oils of Dipterocarpaceae species
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in E. coli BL21(DE3)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes borneol biosynthesis with very high product specificity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DzTPS3 converted GPP to borneol precursor in vitro. 2. GC-MS after dephosphorylation confirmed borneol as the major product. 3. The enzyme displayed high specificity and is a functional BPPS from D. zeylanicus.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Motif filtering; heterologous protein expression in E. coli; in vitro enzyme assay with GPP; alkaline phosphatase treatment; GC-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key enzyme for borneol biosynthesis; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzymatic assay; alkaline phosphatase added to dephosphorylate bornyl diphosphate for GC-MS detection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering high-purity natural borneol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing; RNA-seq; PacBio Iso-Seq; CNGBdb CNP0002104

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase (TPS); monoterpene cyclase; contains conserved RRX8W, DDXXD, and NSE/DTE motifs
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Bornyl diphosphate; borneol detected after alkaline phosphatase dephosphorylation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes geranyl diphosphate to bornyl diphosphate, which yields borneol after dephosphorylation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms immediate precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal monoterpene used for anxiety, pain, anesthesia; contributes to essential oil bioactivity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Essential oils; damar resin and oils of Dipterocarpaceae species
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in E. coli BL21(DE3)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes borneol biosynthesis with very high product specificity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DaTPS3 was functionally validated to produce borneol from GPP in vitro. 2. Product specificity was high with minimal intermediate products. 3. It represents a Dipterocarpus BPPS suitable for borneol pathway reconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Motif filtering; heterologous protein expression in E. coli; in vitro enzyme assay with GPP; alkaline phosphatase treatment; GC-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key enzyme for borneol biosynthesis; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzymatic assay; alkaline phosphatase added to dephosphorylate bornyl diphosphate for GC-MS detection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enzyme for metabolic engineering of borneol and other monoterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing; RNA-seq; PacBio Iso-Seq; CNGBdb CNP0002104

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase (TPS); monoterpene cyclase; contains conserved RRX8W, DDXXD, and NSE/DTE motifs
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Bornyl diphosphate; borneol detected after alkaline phosphatase dephosphorylation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes geranyl diphosphate to bornyl diphosphate, which yields borneol after dephosphorylation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms immediate precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal monoterpene used for anxiety, pain, anesthesia; contributes to essential oil bioactivity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Essential oils; damar resin and oils of Dipterocarpaceae species
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in E. coli BL21(DE3)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes borneol biosynthesis with very high product specificity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DgTPS1 was validated as a BPPS by in vitro assay. 2. GC-MS confirmed borneol formation from GPP after phosphatase treatment. 3. The enzyme showed high specificity relative to previously reported promiscuous BPPS proteins.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Motif filtering; heterologous protein expression in E. coli; in vitro enzyme assay with GPP; alkaline phosphatase treatment; GC-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key enzyme for borneol biosynthesis; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzymatic assay; alkaline phosphatase added to dephosphorylate bornyl diphosphate for GC-MS detection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value enzyme for synthetic biology production of natural borneol
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing; RNA-seq; PacBio Iso-Seq; CNGBdb CNP0002104
